channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Rubbish dumped, 20th July
item.description:
Bags of rubbish dumped in walnut tree road car park

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Tyler Street, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Old mattress dumped on the pavement, 20th July
item.description:
An old mattress has been dumped on the pavement, been there for three days

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Enderby Street, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped beds, 20th July
item.description:
A pile of bed frames and mattresses dumped. We have also noticed rubbish previously reported as fixed simply moved into the adjacent flower garden!

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Alvey Street, Walworth, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bin area over run with flies, 20th July
item.description:
The bottom of the bin is broken and the rubbish is falling straight through to the floor. The area is smothered in flies.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Park Close, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Unannounced roadworks starting at 10pm, 20th July
item.description:
Very noisy roadworks started at 10pm. There is nothing on one.network to show that a permit has been issued and what the works are for.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 203, Woodhouse Road, Friern Barnet, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Old car with advertisement posters, 20th July
item.description:
Looks like an abandoned old vehicle on the side of the road, has been “parked” here since 10th of July

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Winn Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large scale dumping, 20th July
item.description:
Large scale dumping around the skip that has been left there for months.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Kingsland Passage, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned van, 20th July
item.description:
Abandoned van , unsafe position on the road

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Kent House Lane, New Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Outside 36 Platt's Lane, 20th July
item.description:
You've put a shield on the lamp, but as you can see, its too small and light still pours into bedrooms.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Platt's Lane, West Hampstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped caravan, 20th July
item.description:
This caravan has not moved in over 2 years and is taking up parking spaces that taxed and insured vehicles could be utilising, there is no car attached to it so obviously dumped. Please get it removed. Potential for it to be broken into and used for something

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 97-149, Hazel Grove, Perry Vale, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: A board, 20th July
item.description:
Why is this shop allowed it’s A board in the centre of the high street when the rest of us have to stick to the rules. Been reported countless times yet still allowed. Two tier rules?

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 104-108, High Street, Plaistow, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish dumped on the pavement, 20th July
item.description:
On this stretch of pavement there is some sort of cabinet, which some people seem to think is a rubbish bin. Please could you either remove this cabinet or clearly mark it as not being a waste bin.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 515-519, Upper Elmers End Road, Upper Elmers End, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned car at Southdown House, 20th July
item.description:
This car was placed here today and left in front of the electrical room which is a hazard. It is at the side of Southdown House.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Pytchley Road, East Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Another tag, 20th July
item.description:
Tag on utility box

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Tylney Road, Widmore, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Mattress and bed frames, 20th July
item.description:
Someone’s dumped a rug, Mattress and bed frame on the pavement - blocking access for anyone with wheels
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Clifton Crescent, Peckham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dead fox, 20th July
item.description:
Kentish way. Between a gap in the bushes ///into.leaned.gains I think is closest what three words. Picture attached of building inline with dead fox.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Kentish Way, Plaistow, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Continuous flytipping…at either end of Ravenstone Road, 20th July
item.description:
We suffer from extreme “local” fly tipping - this is continuous and it’s not getting any better…infact it appears to be getting worse. I guess this can be confirmed by the council fly tipping maps? In the absence of any enforcement or apparent reduction in volume of fly tipping can we have permanent signs at each end of the street and on each side..pointing out its not normal to do it or acceptable ….we have nothing at the moment.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 31, Ravenstone Road, Hornsey, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: A street light is out but it’s one of the only lights we have, 20th July
item.description:
I have reported this light twice in the last 24 months & it’s not been fixed. It’s one of the only lights making it extremely dark without it! We also have had a spat of burglaries & car break ins. I have cctv but I couldn’t even give it to the police as the light is out & nothing is visible. I pay my council tax & expect to feel safe & know my property is safe but without the light this is impossible it’s outside 29 valence road
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Valence Road, Northumberland Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pot hole, 20th July
item.description:
This pothole is 30cm x 30cm and 20cm deep Almost a sinkhole

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Brockley Park, Honor Oak, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pothole in pavement., 20th July
item.description:
Needs fixing asap as it’s a trip hazard especially in the dark

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lakeswood Road, Poverest, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
